rulings.Case Title: NIVANKA PARANAVITANA VS. CHRISTOPHER TAVARES DEMORES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Gunther Gorse-Greenhut, Christina Gernigan, Udr Western Residential, Inc., Winterland San Francisco Partners, A California Notice Of Motion For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 16, 2012, Line 6, DefendantS Gunther Gorse-Greenhut, Christina Gernigan, Udr Western Residential, Inc. AND Winterland San Francisco Partners, A California Notice Of Motion For Protective Order - Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No tentative ruling. Hearing required. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: 1488 HARRISON STREET OWNERS' ASSOCIATION et al VS. LAURENCE MCAULEY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Response Sand For Sanctions Against X-Deft'S Memo Of P&A
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 16, 2012, Line 6. DEFENDANT LAURENCE MCAULEY'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Response and For Sanctions Against X-Deft'S. Granted. Bonelli Enterprises shall personally serve verified and complete responses to Form Interrogatories, Sets One and Two, Special Interrogatories, Set One, and Requests for Production, Set One, and shall produce all responsive documents no later than March 19, 2012 at 5:00 p.m. All objections, including attorney-client privilege and attorney work product, are waived. For form interrogatories, each response shall include the name, address and telephone number of each identified person and a complete response to each subpart of the form interrogatory. For requests for production, each response shall comply with Code of Civil Procedure 2031.210(a), 2031.220 and 2031.230. For Special Interrogatories, Bonelli Enterprises shall identify all facts, persons and/or documents without incorporation by reference to the document production. Further, Bonelli Enterprises shall provide complete responses to each special interrogatory including identifying all facts, identifying the name, address and telephone number of each person and identifying the date of the document, author(s), recipient(s), address and a brief description of each document. Sanctions of $ 3,843.00 are awarded to Laurence McAuley and shall be paid by Bonelli Enterprises and its counsel no later than April 15, 2012. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 16, 2012, Line 1, DEFENDANT IRA FRIEDMAN Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Req. For Production And Req. For Sanctions In The Amount Of $1446.00 Against Darius Mcree, Precious Mccree, Vannoy Mccree And Robyn Moore;Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Denied, without prejudice. No opposition was filed, but the moving party failed to provide courtesy copies of the moving papers per San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. rulings.Case Title: NIKOLAS LEMOS VS. PROGRESSIVE CHOICE INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Top Compel Further Responses From Progressive Choice Insruance Company Re Interrogatories ; Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 16, 2012, Line 16. PLAINTIFF NIKOLAS LEMOS' Notice Of Motion And Motion Top Compel Further Responses From Progressive Choice Insuance Company Re Interrogatories. The court adopts in full the recommendation and reasoning of Pro Tem Judge Jim Weixel as follows: Granted in part and denied in part. As to Requests for Production Nos. 2, 3, "3" (as mistyped), 8, and 16: The motion is granted, but only to require the production of manuals, indices, office-specific procedures, and region-specific procedures that are applicable to claims that (1) involve property and/or personal injury claims arising from incidents involving two or more passenger vehicles; and (2) arose in San Francisco from 2005 through the present. As to Requests for Production Nos. 4 and 5: The motion is granted only insofar as those requests call for documents concerning training, education, and performance, and as limited by the protective order agreed to by the parties. Production of other portions of the personnel files is too invasive of privacy. As to Requests for Production Nos. 9 and 13: The motion is granted, but only to require the production of documents related to complaints and investigations with, to, or by the Department of Insurance or the Insurance Commissioner pertaining to (1) property and/or personal injury claims arising from incidents involving two or more passenger vehicles; and (2) arose in California from 2005 through the present. As to Requests for Production Nos. 6, 7, 11, 12, 14, 15, and 16, and Form Interrogatory No. 17.1: The motion is granted in full and further responses are compelled. Progressive's arguments about the alleged prejudice to the UIM arbitration are not well taken. The UIM statute specifically provides for discovery to proceed in a civil action notwithstanding the pendency of a UIM arbitration proceeding. Insurance Code 11580.2(f)(7). Further responses shall be served within 30 calendar days of the entry of this order, without objections except as to privilege. If Progressive contends that any responsive document is privileged, Progressive is ordered to produce a privilege log stating the nature of the document, its date, its author, the recipient(s) thereof, its sequence or identification number if any, and the privilege claimed as to that document. (Code Civ. Proc. §2031.240(b); Best Products, Inc. v. Superior Court (2004) 119 Cal.App.4th 1181, 1188). If it turns out that a particular document does not exist or cannot be found, those facts shall be stated in the response. Plaintiff's request for sanctions of $1,000.00 is granted, with Progressive (but not its counsel) ordered to pay that amount to Plaintiff's counsel within 20 calendar days of the entry of an order on this motion. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: NIKOLAS LEMOS VS. PROGRESSIVE CHOICE INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Stay Discovery Pending Resolution Of Uim Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 16, 2012, Line 16. DEFENDANT PROGRESSIVE CHOICE INSURANCE COMPANY'S Notice Of Motion To Stay Discovery Pending Resolution Of Uim Arbitration. The court adopts if full the recommendation and reasoning of Pro Tem Judge Jim Weixel as follows: The motion is denied. The stay is overly broad and would undermine the usual presumption that discovery will be allowed to proceed in any sequence. (Code of Civil Procedure 2019.020(a)). Progressive's concerns about the disclosure of privileged or otherwise protected information in a few unspecified instances do not outweigh this policy and do not justify an overall stay on discovery. Code of Civil Procedure 1281.4 pertains to contractual arbitration generally and by its terms pertains only to arbitration that has been compelled by a court. Also, the UIM statute specifically provides that "[n]othing in this section limits the rights of any party to discovery in any action pending or that may hereafter be pending in any court." (Insurance Code 11580.2(f)(7)). This more specific statute prevails over the more general provisions of 1281.4. (Code of Civil Procedure 1859; Collection Bureau of San Jose v. Rumsey (2000) 24 Cal.4th 301, 310). The UIM statute provides for discovery to proceed in a civil action without regard to the pendency of the UIM arbitration, and follows the general policy of allowing discovery to proceed in an action notwithstanding the pendency of another proceeding. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: STANLEY SHEN VS. ALICE TOY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Ofmotion And Motion For Sanctions Under Ccp 128.7; Memo Of P&A
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 16, 2012, Line 13. PLAINTIFF STANLEY SHEN'S Notice Of motion And Motion For Sanctions Under Ccp 128.7. Denied. The moving party failed to provide courtesy copies of the moving papers as required by Local Rule 2.6B. Moreover, it appears that the motion is procedurally improper since defendants were not given the required 21 day safe harbor period to withdraw their demurrer. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: STANLEY SHEN VS. ALICE TOY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 16, 2012, Line 13. DEFENDANTS ALICE TOY AND HA HUYNH'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overuled. While the court did not receive courtesy copies of the opposition papers as required by Local Rule 2.6B which made it harder for the court to decide this matter, the demurrer appears without merit for two reasons. First, the order filed January 6 overruled defendants' demurrers to the first five claims and successive demurrers are not permitted. Second, Mr. Shen alleges that he was personally damages and he appears to be a party to the lease dated October 25, 2010. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GIUSEPPE TERMINNIELLO VS. BMW OF NORTH AMERICA, LLC, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Compliance Further Request For Form Interrogatories; Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 16, 2012, Line 5, PLAINTIFF GIUSEPPE TERMINNIELLO'S Motion To Compel Compliance Further Request For Form Interrogatories; Request For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion is GRANTED. Defendant's objections to this interrogatory state that "counsel is presently unaware of specific facts, witnesses or documents which would be responsive to this particular interrogatory at this time." Defendant's response states: "Responding Party's answer was filed based upon pleading practices in the State of California." Defendant argues in its opposition separate statement that it is "presently unaware of specific facts witnesses or documents which would be responsive to this particular interrogatory at this time." If there are no facts upon which defendant bases its denial of the material allegations of the complaint, and there are no facts upon which defendant bases each affirmative defense it pleaded, then plaintiff is entitled to a simple, verified response stating "none." Otherwise, Defendant should state the facts that support any of defendants' denials of the material allegations of the complaint and affirmative defenses. Defendant shall provide plaintiff with a complete verified response to Form Interrogatory No. 15.1 within 20 days of entry of this order. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: GIUSEPPE TERMINNIELLO VS. BMW OF NORTH AMERICA, LLC, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses By Defendant;Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 16, 2012, Line 5, PLAINTIFF GIUSEPPE TERMINNIELLO'S Motion To Compel Further Responses By Defendant;Request For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion is GRANTED IN PART and DENIED IN PART. Defendant's responses to Special Interrogatory Nos. 12, 13, 16, 23, 24 referring Plaintiff to the documents produced are sufficient given the small volume of documents (51 pages total). Defendant's response to Interrogatory Nos. 33, 34 and 35 and 36, and 39 are also sufficient. Defendant's response to Interrogatories 14, 19, 21, 25 and 30 by referring to documents is inadequate. Those requests ask Defendant to identify witnesses who may or may not be listed in the documents defendants have referenced. Defendants' answers to Interrogatory Nos. 37 and 40 are also noncompliant. They consist in total of the conclusory statement that "the subject vehicle was conformed to warranty" and are devoid of any facts to support this assertion. Within 20 days of entry of this order Defendants shall provide supplemental verified responses to Interrogatories 14, 19, 21, 25, 30, 37, and 40. Plaintiffs' request for sanctions is DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: GIUSEPPE TERMINNIELLO VS. BMW OF NORTH AMERICA, LLC, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Compliance To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents From Defendant; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 16, 2012, Line 5, PLAINTIFF GIUSEPPE TERMINNIELLO'S Notice Of Motion To Compel Compliance To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents From Defendant; Request For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion is GRANTED IN PART and DENIED IN PART. Defendant's objection to Request No. 4 is well taken. Documents concerning BMW's warranty reimbursements to its dealer are not likely to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence given that the Song-Beverly Act's provisions regarding restitution call for "reasonable repair . . . costs actually incurred by the buyer." Civ. Code Section 1793.2. Defendant has complied with the requirements of the Code with respect to Request No 9. Defendant's objections with regard to Request Nos. 10, 12, 13, and 14 are not well taken. Defendant argues in its opposition separate statement that it has produced all "relevant documents" (not "responsive" documents) that are responsive to these categories of requests, but has not provided a statement of compliance by a representative of its client, verified under penalty of perjury, meeting the requirement of C.C.P. Section 2031.220. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK
